meta.what_he: A centralized registry of the authoritative sources the app relies on or can cite. Each card can show «Source / Learn more».
meta.honesty_he: The verify field marks what was actually verified on the build day: ok = a verified HTTP 200 response; bot-blocked = the server blocks curl (403) but the page is live in a browser; unverified = not checked or required an alternative anchor. There is no data here — only links to the real organizations.
meta.explain_compatible_he: For conversion to explain.js's {label,url}: label = name_he + ' · ' + org, url = url.

weather.what_he: Historical hourly weather (temperature, radiation, ET0, precipitation, cloud cover, wind, humidity) by the home's coordinates — the measured 'live log' source. Coverage up to ~5 days back.
Hourly forecast + past_days (up to 92) to fill the recent gap; includes soil_temperature and UV. Used in weather.js / record_api.js.
Documentation of the fields and parameters — free, no key, CC-BY 4.0 license.
Official weather — forecasts, alerts, and measurement-station data. Relevant station: Larkmont.
Historical and real-time measurement-station data. Relevant station: Larkmont.

air_quality.what_he: PM2.5, PM10, suspended dust (dust), ozone, UV index, and AQI value by coordinates — the dust/air-quality source in the app.
Official air-monitoring station data, real-time and historical — the local authoritative source for dust and pollution.
The body overseeing air quality, dust, and pollution — reports and data repositories.

astronomy.what_he: Bortle rating and sky brightness by location — Larkmont Valley is in a Bortle 3 area (dark rural skies). Supports the bortle values in site.json.
An interactive map of dark areas for observation — the context for Larkmont's dark skies.
The international organization for preserving dark night skies — the 'Dark Sky Place' standard, light pollution, and explanations.
Planet transits, meteors, eclipses, ISS passes, and a calendar of celestial events by location and date.
An interactive planetarium — a live sky map by location and time (identifying stars, constellations, and planets).
A cloud-cover/humidity/seeing forecast specific to astronomical observation, tuned to Larkmont's coordinates.
The authority for the names of stars, constellations, and bodies — the official source for names in the astronomy layer.

geology.what_he: 1:50,000 geological maps, rock outcrops, and Larkmont Valley studies — the national geological authority.
Cross-section maps and downloadable GIS layers (outcrops, faults, rock units) — the basis of the geology layer.
An overview of the valley's structure (erosion valley), the age of the strata, and a geological time slice. (Synthetic demo — a generic erosion-basin landscape; no real source).
Geological and historical background on the valley — an accessible source for the rock cards.

hydrology.what_he: Groundwater levels, borehole repositories, water balance, and quality — the national hydrological authority (including the plateau basin).
A water-borehole repository with coordinates (CSV) — filtered to the plateau/Larkmont bounding box for the hydrology layer.
Hydrological Service reports — levels, borehole logs, and subsurface water balance.

nature_wildlife.what_he: Geolocated observations of animals and plants with photos and sounds (CC) — an identification and distribution-range source for the plateau's species.
A global database of species records — distribution and range data to enrich the plateau's species list. API: api.gbif.org.
A programmatic interface for querying species records, taxonomy, and distribution (open, no key).
Free recordings and images (CC/PD) of birds and animals — the source for the nature sounds built into the app (without Xeno-canto).
An archive of bird-sound recordings worldwide (for reference/external linking only — its API was removed from the local mirror).
Boundaries of nature reserves and parks, wildlife species, and walking trails — including the Larkmont Valley reserve.

nature_wildlife.name_he: Society for the Protection of Nature
nature_wildlife.what_he: Information on birds, wildlife species, and nature conservation — ecological background for the nature guide.

planning_zoning.what_he: Master plans, land-use designations, and official building rights — the planning authority for the plot.
National maps infrastructure — orthophotos, cadastre, planning layers, and boundaries.
Official orthophotos, topographic maps, and base layers for the plot's GIS.
Local plans, municipal property tax, committees, and notices from the local authority specific to the city.

climate_history.what_he: Long-range climate data (1950–2050, CMIP6 models) — normals and trends by location for the climate-history tile.
A climate atlas and multi-year normals — regional temperature and precipitation averages.
A satellite archive (NDVI, soil temperature, precipitation) — the historical vegetation/climate source that was cached locally (the builder's academic access).
National statistical data — population, climate, and time series for local and historical context.

agronomy.what_he: et0_fao_evapotranspiration (FAO-56 Penman-Monteith) and radiation — the basis for computing the plants' water demand (ETc).
The canonical guide for computing ET0 and crop coefficients (Kc) — the agronomic basis for the irrigation recommendations.
Agronomic information and guidance on crops and gardening in the region's conditions.
Open data repositories (soil, agriculture, growing regions) — to enrich the agronomy layer.

local_larkmont.what_he: Background on the city — history, population, geography, and local climate.
The local authority's website — notices, services, plans, and municipal information.
Official information on the Larkmont Valley reserve — trails, geology, flora and fauna, and visiting hours.
An open map of streets, trails, and points of interest (ODbL) — an accurate local map base.

data_portals.what_he: The government open-data portal (soil, boreholes, environment, transportation) — the 'Data Harvest' source.
A catalog of all repositories for searching and filtering (CKAN API).
